title: Runtime Modes v1
description: Maps user intent modes to workflow classes, gate sequences, and autonomy defaults. The runtime engine selects mode based on Operator input and routes accordingly.

chat.description: Conversational mode. No build or research pipeline. Operator handles directly or fast-paths to transcription.
triggers: User sends conversational message without explicit build/research intent

build.description: Standard build mode. Full gate sequence with planning and verification.
triggers: User requests code change, tool creation, UI modification, or ops fix
User selects a build-class intent mode

research_build.description: Research-first mode. Research phase runs before planning. May terminate after research if research_only.
triggers: User requests investigation or research before building
Orchestrator classifies task as requiring research phase

mode_selection_rules.rule: Operator classifies user intent into a mode. If ambiguous, default to research_build.
Mode selection is immutable once workflow enters classifying state. Mode change requires new workflow.
research_only workflows in research_build mode skip planning and building phases. Flow: idle -> classifying -> researching -> research_review -> complete.
Autonomy mode may be overridden by user preference or policy at workflow start. Policy override takes precedence.
